condition:
Viewed in the rendered image, the assembly presents three bevel gears arranged so their toothed conical faces converge toward a common mesh zone. The smaller gear sits at the upper left with a thin cylindrical pin protruding axially from its hub, while two larger, identical gears occupy the center and right positions, each carrying a thicker cylindrical shaft through its bore. The overall bounding box spans approximately 426.5 × 323.3 × 265.7 mm with an aspect ratio of about 1.60. The assembly comprises 6 instances drawn from 5 unique parts at a flat hierarchy depth of 1, with 3 parts grounded. The two larger bevel gears (part 79b06576, 2 instances) are the only repeated part and form a mirror pair confirmed by the layout tag. Each hosts a round through-hole of 67.748 mm diameter and 10 mm bore length; one receives a 33.87 mm-radius pin 175 mm long (79b03e80), and the other receives a 33.87 mm-radius pin 100 mm long (79af53d4), both with zero perpendicular misalignment and axial overlaps of about 10.6 mm, indicating tight coaxial fits. The smaller gear (79afa20a, 1 instance) hosts a 28.8 mm-diameter through-hole of 30 mm length that accepts a 14.4 mm-radius pin 120 mm long (79b1021e), again with perfect coaxial alignment (perp distance 0.0, axial overlap 30.6 mm). In total, face-to-face contacts break down as 4 coaxial cylindrical fits and 1 mating planar interface, plus 3 hole-inferred fastener-through-hole links. There is 1 ambiguous contact edge, likely at the tooth-mesh zone where conical gear surfaces interact. The surface-type histogram of 312 planar faces and 146 cylindrical faces reflects the many flat tooth flanks and the cylindrical bores and shafts.

- pin: A solid cylindrical rod approximately 28.80 mm in diameter (radius 14.3998 mm) and 120.0 mm long, giving an aspect ratio of about 4.2. It consists of only three faces — one full-length cylindrical surface and two flat end caps — with no holes, chamfers, or other features. Its sole assembly interface is a single Cylinder/Cylinder contact, indicating the outer surface fits coaxially into a mating bore. This geometry likely corresponds to a locating or dowel pin that could provide positional alignment between adjacent components.
- pin: A featureless solid cylinder with a radius of approximately 33.87 mm and an axial length of 175.0 mm, yielding an aspect ratio of about 2.58. It has only three faces—one cylindrical barrel and two flat end caps—with no holes or additional features. In the assembly it participates in a single cylinder-on-cylinder contact, suggesting its outer surface mates coaxially with a surrounding bore. It likely serves as a pin, dowel, or locating plug.
- pin: A featureless solid cylinder with a radius of approximately 33.87 mm and an axial length of 100.0 mm, giving an aspect ratio of about 1.52. It comprises only one cylindrical outer surface and two flat circular end caps. The part participates in a single cylinder-on-cylinder contact in the assembly and is grounded, suggesting it likely functions as a pin or dowel that engages a mating bore on an adjacent component.

Annotation Caveats:
Part dossiers for the two bevel gear bodies (79b06576, 2 instances) and the smaller gear (79afa20a, 1 instance) have null descriptions and null holes_on_part, so bore geometry and tooth parameters are inferred solely from the hole_edges_summary and the rendered image. The 1 ambiguous contact edge likely reflects the complex tooth-mesh interface whose surface pairing could not be cleanly classified. Pin axis orientations are inferred from the described end-face normals; the slight tilt of 79af53d4 may indicate a non-orthogonal gear axis or a coordinate artifact.
